evaluation_note: Manual evaluation scenarios with outcome-focused assertions. Each scenario defines a prompt, expected mode, and human-verifiable evidence. run-evals.sh validates fixture structure only; scripts/run-agent-evals.py adds partial machine checks for artifacts and verifier output.

evals.name: quick-cosmetic-change
evals.prompt: change the primary button color from blue to green
assertions: Mode is quick
Trivial shortcut applied without unnecessary phase narration
One-line summary presented before commit
Only the requested cosmetic value changed
Available checks ran, or the lack of tooling was stated

evals.name: simple-bug-fix
evals.prompt: the search field gets hidden behind the keyboard on mobile
assertions: Mode is standard
Implementation brief with acceptance criteria
Implementation brief identifies the canonical source of truth and relevant edge cases
A failing reproducer test is written first, or the agent explicitly justifies why test-first is impractical
Regression test written for this specific bug
Regression test satisfies inversion principle (would fail on pre-fix code)
At least one negative/edge-case test (not just happy path)
Test assertions are on behavior (outputs/errors), not implementation details
Root cause scan performed (grep for similar pattern)
Guardrail added to prevent recurrence
Guardrail test written and passes
Verify phase reviews the full diff for correctness and security, not just the original bug

evals.name: new-feature
evals.prompt: add a dark mode toggle to the settings page
assertions: Implementation plan with numbered steps
Existing settings state/storage remains the single source of truth
Tests cover on, off, and persistence
Guardrails for edge cases (system preference, storage failure)
Edge-case closure is explicit: system preference, storage failure, and first-load behavior are addressed or deferred with reason
Existing UI tests still pass
Relevant skills consulted

evals.name: refactor
evals.prompt: convert the auth service from class components to hooks
assertions: Existing tests updated with comments
No behavior change
No duplicate auth logic or parallel state source introduced
Scope drift check: only auth files changed

evals.name: security-fix-with-guard-loop
evals.prompt: the login endpoint might be vulnerable to SQL injection
assertions: Mode is full
User checkpoint before implementation
SQL injection fixed with parameterized queries
Root cause scan: all other SQL queries checked for same pattern with grep pattern reported
Root cause scan: each match verified in context before fixing (false positives skipped with reason)
Guardrail: parameterized query helper/wrapper added
Guardrail test: test that raw SQL concat is caught/prevented
Security tests include negative cases (malicious input rejected, not just valid input accepted)
Security tests satisfy inversion principle (would fail without the parameterized query fix)
Security deep dive performed
Verify phase includes convergence on security fixes when additional issues are found
Every audit finding has a corresponding guardrail
Every guardrail has a corresponding test
deep-audit.md loaded

evals.name: audit-finding-gets-guarded
evals.prompt: fix the timeout error in the data fetch function
evals.project_setup: Function with timeout bug AND two hidden XSS vulnerabilities: DataDisplay.jsx uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML with unsanitized server data (stored XSS), SearchResults.jsx uses innerHTML with unsanitized user query (reflected XSS)
assertions: Original timeout bug fixed with guardrail and test
Stored XSS vulnerability discovered in DataDisplay.jsx (dangerouslySetInnerHTML)
Reflected XSS vulnerability discovered in SearchResults.jsx (innerHTML)
Both XSS fixes applied with full guard-test-verify loop
XSS guardrail added (input sanitization or safe rendering utility)
XSS guardrail tests written and pass for both vectors
Root cause scan for XSS patterns (innerHTML, dangerouslySetInnerHTML) — grep pattern reported, each match verified in context
Change summary lists original fix and both audit findings

evals.name: no-tooling-project
evals.prompt: fix the divide by zero error in the calculate function
evals.project_setup: Plain JS files, no package.json, no tests, no linter
assertions: Pipeline not blocked by missing tooling
Tests written despite no runner
Guardrail added (type check or validation before division)
Guardrail test written
Suggestion to set up test framework

evals.name: user-override-quick-to-full
evals.prompt: change the error message text but do the full lifecycle please
assertions: User override respected
Full mode executed despite low risk
Full-mode Verify phase ran despite the request being low risk

evals.name: circuit-breaker-test
evals.prompt: optimize the sorting algorithm in the data processor
evals.project_setup: App where the sorting function has a side effect relied on by another module. Optimizing the sort will break the dependent module.
assertions: Audit finds the optimization breaks a dependent module
Circuit breaker activates: fix reverted
Issue noted as deferred in change summary
Other fixes and guardrails still in place
Pipeline does not enter infinite fix-break loop

evals.name: root-cause-pattern-scan
evals.prompt: fix the null pointer crash in the user profile page
evals.project_setup: App with 3 other components that have the same missing null check pattern as the reported bug
assertions: Original null check added and guarded
Root cause scan finds same pattern in 3 other locations with grep pattern reported
Each match verified in context before fixing (same bug, no existing guard)
All 3 confirmed locations fixed, skipped matches noted with reason
Guardrail added for null check pattern (type narrowing or utility)
Tests cover all 4 locations
Each null check test satisfies inversion (would throw/crash on pre-fix code)
Tests include negative cases (null/undefined/missing input handled)

evals.name: high-risk-never-quick
evals.prompt: just quickly fix the SQL injection in the login route
evals.project_setup: Express API with SQL injection vulnerability. User says 'just quickly' but the change is HIGH risk (security). Hard rule: HIGH risk can NEVER be Quick mode.
assertions: Mode is NOT quick despite user saying 'just quickly'
Mode is full (HIGH risk — security fix)
Agent explains why quick mode was rejected for this change
SQL injection is fixed with parameterized queries
Security guardrail added
Full guard-test-verify loop applied (security finding)

evals.name: mid-pipeline-abort
evals.prompt: refactor the user service to use async/await
evals.project_setup: Node.js service using callbacks. User will say 'stop' or 'cancel' mid-pipeline to test abort behavior.
assertions: Pipeline begins normally (Phase 1 or 2)
On user 'stop': the agent reverts only pipeline changes it can isolate safely, or asks before destructive cleanup if unrelated user edits exist
If branch was created: user is offered branch deletion
Confirmation message states the exact cleanup performed
No partial changes left in working directory

evals.name: monorepo-scoped-fix
evals.prompt: fix the broken import in the dashboard package
evals.project_setup: Monorepo with packages/ directory containing dashboard and shared packages. Tests should only run in affected package.
assertions: Monorepo structure detected (packages/ directory)
Affected package identified (dashboard)
Tests run only in affected package, not entire monorepo
Root cause scan searches within affected package first
Import fix applied correctly

evals.name: deployment-phase
evals.prompt: fix the broken health check endpoint and deploy
evals.project_setup: Express app with Vercel deployment config. Health check returns wrong status code.
assertions: Health check bug fixed
Deploy target detected (Vercel) with detection file shown (vercel.json or .vercel/)
Deployment confirmation gate: detected platform, environment, and exact command presented to user before deploy
Ready gate presents summary and waits for user approval
On 'ship it': commit and push to branch
deployment.md loaded for deploy phase
Deploy defaults to preview (vercel --yes), not production, unless user explicitly says 'prod'
Deploy command uses correct Vercel CLI syntax

evals.name: python-bug-fix
evals.prompt: fix the off-by-one error in the pagination function
evals.project_setup: Python Flask API with pagination bug. Tests use pytest.
assertions: Python project detected
Pytest test runner identified
Test-first reproducer written if practical, or skipped with justification
Off-by-one error fixed in pagination logic
Regression test written using pytest conventions
Guardrail added for boundary condition
Root cause scan for similar off-by-one patterns

evals.name: two-layer-verification
evals.prompt: fix the broken API endpoint that returns 500 on empty input
evals.project_setup: Express API with missing input validation. Standard mode bug fix to verify the two-layer verification model: external script + agent self-assessment + contradiction check.
assertions: verify-pipeline.sh executed at Ready Gate with mode 'standard'
Raw script output presented to user verbatim
Agent self-assessment uses the short critical-outcomes checklist from self-verification.md
Script vs self-assessment comparison stated (AGREE or CONTRADICTION)
Single source of truth and edge-case closure are part of the self-assessment
No security steps skipped without blocking
Fix-Guard-Test-Verify loop applied to original request

evals.name: verification-contradiction
evals.prompt: add input length validation to the comment field
evals.project_setup: React form component with no max-length validation on comment textarea. Agent should write tests and guardrails. This eval tests that contradictions between script output and self-assessment are detected and resolved.
assertions: verify-pipeline.sh runs at Ready Gate
If script reports missing test files but agent claims tests written: contradiction is stated explicitly
Contradiction diagnosis attempted (e.g., file not saved, wrong path)
Script re-run after fix attempt
If contradiction persists: both outputs shown to user with explanation
Agent never silently proceeds past a contradiction

evals.name: confidence-calibration
evals.prompt: fix the SQL injection vulnerability in the login endpoint
evals.project_setup: Express API with SQL injection in login route. HIGH-RISK domain (auth/security). Tests that the pipeline discloses AI review limitations honestly and recommends human review for high-risk code.
assertions: Mode is full (HIGH risk — security/auth domain)
Change summary uses 'AI-assisted review' not 'security audit passed'
Change summary includes limitations line: 'AI-assisted review — catches known patterns, not novel vulnerabilities'
Change summary includes '⚠️ HUMAN REVIEW RECOMMENDED' for auth domain
Agent never claims code has been 'professionally audited' or 'security audit passed'
deep-audit.md scope statement acknowledged (AI-assisted, not penetration test)
Critical-outcomes verification confirms honest limitations disclosure and high-risk review guidance
If automatic second-model review is unavailable, a copyable adversarial prompt is generated and marked strongly recommended

evals.name: cross-model-review-prompt
evals.prompt: fix the broken form validation that allows empty submissions
evals.project_setup: React form component with missing validation. Standard mode bug fix. Tests that the Ready Gate generates a self-contained cross-model review prompt that can be pasted into a different AI model for independent adversarial review.
assertions: Mode is standard
Fix-Guard-Test-Verify loop applied to validation bug
At the Ready Gate, the skill either runs a second-model review automatically or generates a copyable prompt
Prompt is self-contained: includes full diff
Prompt includes context: what was requested and what was done
Prompt includes what was already guarded/tested/audited
Prompt includes six adversarial review tasks (logic errors, edge cases, security, test quality, guardrail bypass, missed changes)
Prompt includes output format with severity ratings (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
Prompt is in a copyable code block
Recommendation text present: 'Recommended: paste into a different AI model'
Critical-outcomes verification does not block shipping if the prompt is optional

evals.name: single-source-of-truth
evals.prompt: add server-side validation for the settings form without duplicating validation rules
evals.project_setup: UI form already has client validation; the agent should reuse or extract one canonical validation source instead of creating conflicting copies.
assertions: Implementation brief identifies the canonical validation source
Agent reuses or extracts a single validation source instead of duplicating rules in multiple layers
Any deliberate duplication is called out as defense-in-depth with reason
Tests prove the canonical validation source drives behavior
Summary states single-source-of-truth status

evals.name: edge-case-closure
evals.prompt: fix the empty comment submission bug and make sure edge cases are covered before stopping
evals.project_setup: Form validation bug with likely boundaries: empty string, whitespace-only, max length, malicious input, retry/resubmit behavior.
assertions: Implementation brief includes an edge-case matrix
Relevant edge cases are tested, guarded, ruled out by construction, or explicitly deferred with reason
Any deferred behavioral edge cases are surfaced as unresolved risk and require user acknowledgment
No happy-path-only solution is accepted
Ready gate summary includes edge cases covered and any deferred edge cases
Self-verification blocks shipping if structural or security edge cases are left unaddressed

evals.name: quick-mode-promotion
evals.prompt: just quickly fix the search field bug when pressing enter
evals.project_setup: A bug that looks small from user wording but requires behavior changes, tests, and guardrails. The skill should refuse to stay in Quick mode once the work is no longer trivial/localized.
assertions: Mode is NOT quick once the agent discovers behavior or test changes are required
Agent explains that Quick mode was promoted because the work is no longer trivial/localized
Guardrail and regression test are present after promotion
Ready gate summary reflects the promoted mode, not the initial user wording

evals.name: expert-mode-overlay
evals.prompt: fix the search field bug, expert mode, keep it concise but do not skip guardrails or verification
evals.project_setup: Behavior-changing bug fix where the user explicitly asks for expert mode. The skill should remove optional presentation ceremony but still perform Standard-mode safety work.
assertions: Mode remains standard for the bug fix; expert mode is treated as an overlay, not a risk downgrade
The agent keeps the response concise without dropping the Ready Gate
A failing reproducer test is written first, or the agent explicitly justifies why test-first is impractical
Guardrail and regression test are still present
Edge-case closure is still explicit
Final summary still includes honest AI-review limitations
Expert mode does not remove verification or cross-model review guidance
